clusters.title: Sydney’s UNSW overtakes University of Melbourne in global rankings for first time
clusters.neutral_headline: The Sydney university ranked Australia’s best for the first time
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian Australia, The Age

items.title: Sydney’s UNSW overtakes University of Melbourne in global rankings for first time
items.standfirst: University of New South Wales ranks 19th in QS World University Rankings, with nine Australian universities achieving record-high positionsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe University of New South Wales has overtaken the University of Melbourne for the first time in a prestigious world university rankings list.UNSW ranked 19th in the QS World University Rankings, an improvement of one place for the Sydney university on last year’s result and 30 places since 2017. Continue reading...
items.content: University of New South Wales ranks 19th in QS World University Rankings, with nine Australian universities achieving record-high positionsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe University of New South Wales has overtaken the University of Melbourne for the first time in a prestigious world university rankings list.UNSW ranked 19th in the QS World University Rankings, an improvement of one place for the Sydney university on last year’s result and 30 places since 2017. Continue reading...

items.title: The Sydney university ranked Australia’s best for the first time
items.standfirst: The University of NSW has dethroned the prestigious University of Melbourne as Australia’s best university in rankings released on Thursday.
items.content: The University of NSW has dethroned the prestigious University of Melbourne as Australia’s best university in rankings released on Thursday.

items.title: University of Melbourne toppled from top spot in global rankings
items.standfirst: The prestigious university has been outranked by a Sydney rival for the first time, amid mixed performance across Australia’s historically high-performing tertiary sector.
items.content: The prestigious university has been outranked by a Sydney rival for the first time, amid mixed performance across Australia’s historically high-performing tertiary sector.
